VAL-QBUILD-005.title: Add multiple predicates - shared variable names visually indicate a join

steps.observed: Predicate browser showed Relations, Functions, Probabilistic groups

steps.action: Click PeakReported to add first predicate
steps.expected: PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3) added to query
steps.observed: PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3) added, variables show as clickable tokens

steps.action: Click TermInStudyTFIDF to add second predicate
steps.observed: TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6) added alongside PeakReported

steps.action: Click variable v3 in PeakReported (4th parameter)
steps.observed: textbox 'Rename variable v3' appeared in place of the token

steps.action: Type 'v4' and press Enter to rename v3 to v4
steps.observed: v3 renamed to v4; all 3 v4 tokens now have CSS class 'vqb-var-token--shared' with blue background highlight

evidence.domEvidence: document.querySelectorAll('.vqb-var-token--shared') returned 3 elements all with text 'v4', confirming visual join indication

VAL-QBUILD-007.title: Context-aware suggestions displayed after predicates added

steps.action: Reload page and select neurosynth engine
Wait 3 seconds for debounced suggestions fetch
steps.observed: Suggestions panel shows IDENTIFIERS and BASE SYMBOLS sections with 76 suggestion chips (PeakReported, SelectedStudy, Study, TermInStudyTFIDF, Voxel, functions, etc.)

evidence.domEvidence: No 'No suggestions available' text present; 76 chip elements found via document.querySelectorAll('[class*=chip]')

VAL-QBUILD-008.title: Click suggestion chip adds predicate with proper variable names

steps.action: With PeakReported in query and suggestions visible, take before screenshot
Click 'TermInStudyTFIDF' suggestion chip in IDENTIFIERS section
steps.expected: TermInStudyTFIDF added with proper variable names
steps.observed: TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6) added to query - proper placeholder variable names, not empty parentheses

steps.expected: ans(v, v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6) :- PeakReported(v, v1, v2, v3), TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6).
steps.observed: Datalog matches expected output with all variables populated

evidence.network: Suggestion chip click triggered lookupSymbol() and added TermInStudyTFIDF with v4, v5, v6 variables (visible in Datalog: TermInStudyTFIDF(v4, v5, v6))

VAL-QBUILD-011.title: Variable binding visualization - user can create shared variable bindings

steps.action: After rename operation in VAL-QBUILD-005 flow, check for shared variable CSS class
steps.expected: Shared variable tokens show visual distinction (color/highlight)
steps.observed: CSS class 'vqb-var-token--shared' applied to all 3 v4 token occurrences (1 in 'Find' header, 1 in PeakReported, 1 in TermInStudyTFIDF) with blue background highlight

evidence.domEvidence: document.querySelectorAll('.vqb-var-token--shared') returned [{text:'v4',class:'vqb-var-token vqb-var-token--shared vqb-var-token--editable'}, {text:'v4',...}, {text:'v4',...}] - 3 shared tokens confirmed

frictions.description: The suggestion chips use class 'suggestions-chip' but don't appear in agent-browser snapshot -i output because they're not in the accessibility tree as standard interactive elements. Required using JavaScript eval to click them.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser eval to run JavaScript that found and clicked the button.suggestions-chip element directly.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. The fix-query-builder-ux commit successfully addressed all tested behaviors: (1) variable rename UI opens inline text input, (2) renaming to a matching variable applies 'vqb-var-token--shared' CSS class for visual join indication, (3) suggestions panel shows context-aware chips after predicates are added (not 'No suggestions available'), and (4) clicking a suggestion chip adds the predicate with proper placeholder variable names.
